suggestedPatch.target: This itinerary works because it pins down specific spots upfront, so your group stops debating and starts showing up.
suggestedPatch.before: This itinerary works because it pins down specific spots upfront, so your group stops debating and starts showing up.
suggestedPatch.after: I've organized this trip four times now, and the only version that didn't implode was the one where I booked everything before asking.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Reads like a content-marketing template with 'TL;DR,' 'Key takeaways,' and FAQ scaffolding; voice is generic travel-blog editorial.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 5/10 — Some mild opinions surface (skip Instagram spots, rentals beat hotels) but most claims are safe consensus travel advice.

suggestedPatch.target: Oahu's best group-friendly activities are spaced out, don't overload each day.
suggestedPatch.before: Oahu's best group-friendly activities are spaced out, don't overload each day.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip Pearl Harbor unless someone in your group actually cares—most groups I've taken found it a somber downer that kills the day's momentum.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 5/10: Some mild opinions surface (skip Instagram spots, rentals beat hotels) but most claims are safe consensus travel advice.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — A couple of decent lines ('unpaid travel agent,' 'end-of-trip math meltdowns') but mostly predictable travel prose.

suggestedPatch.target: Fewer 3 a.m. 'what are we doing tomorrow' texts; more time actually in the water.
suggestedPatch.before: Fewer 3 a.m. 'what are we doing tomorrow' texts; more time actually in the water.
suggestedPatch.after: Kept the group chat quieter than a Tuesday at Matsumoto's.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: A couple of decent lines ('unpaid travel agent,' 'end-of-trip math meltdowns') but mostly predictable travel prose.

evidence: Point of view vs balanced survey 6/10 — Takes some stances (rentals over hotels, book early) but often hedges with 'depends on your group.'

suggestedPatch.target: Central Waikiki works best if your group wants nightlife; locals, however, will take Ala Moana rentals to cut both costs and the chaos of Waikiki sidewalk crowds.
suggestedPatch.before: Central Waikiki works best if your group wants nightlife; locals, however, will take Ala Moana rentals to cut both costs and the chaos of Waikiki sidewalk crowds.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip Waikiki hotels—they're a tax on laziness. Book the Ala Moana rental.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs balanced survey" scored 6/10: Takes some stances (rentals over hotels, book early) but often hedges with 'depends on your group.'

evidence: Quotable to a friend 4/10 — Few memorable lines; the 'unpaid travel agent' and 'indecisive friend won't argue with malasadas' are the only candidates.

suggestedPatch.target: Plan hard early, then leave mornings loose—that's the trick for groups of six who all want different things.
suggestedPatch.before: Plan hard early, then leave mornings loose—that's the trick for groups of six who all want different things.
suggestedPatch.after: The group organiser (that's probably you) becomes the unpaid travel agent until somebody books Hanauma Bay.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable to a friend" scored 4/10: Few memorable lines; the 'unpaid travel agent' and 'indecisive friend won't argue with malasadas' are the only candidates.

suggestedPatch.before: Coordinating a 7-day Oahu group trip is chaos until you get specific.
